title: Plate Layout Editor

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: A plate layout defines which constructs go in which wells. The layout editor lets you create, edit, and reuse layouts across experiments.

subsections.heading: Click Selection
subsections.content: Click individual wells to select them. Hold Ctrl/Cmd to add to selection, Shift to select ranges.

subsections.heading: Row/Column Selection
subsections.content: Click row letters (A-H) or column numbers (1-12) to select entire rows or columns.

subsections.heading: Pattern Fill
subsections.content: Use the pattern fill tool to quickly assign constructs in regular patterns (alternating rows, checkerboard, etc.).

sections.heading: Well Types
sections.content: Wells can be assigned as: Sample (construct + ligand), Negative Control (no template), Blank (background correction), or Empty.

sections.heading: Replicates
sections.content: The calculator recommends replicate counts based on desired precision. Spread replicates across rows and columns to minimize systematic effects.

why_it_matters: A well-designed plate layout maximizes precision by ensuring all constructs have proper controls and sufficient replicates for statistical analysis.
